name: Nomina
classification_notes: The specimen text and proportions suggest a humanist sans-serif with open apertures and warm, readable letterforms suited to body text and editorial use. No explicit classification is given on the page, but the range of weights and variable format points to a contemporary humanist design.
summary: A humanist sans-serif from Tokotype offering eight weights with matching italics and a variable font format for both upright and italic styles.
description: Nomina spans eight weights from ExtraLight to Black, each with a corresponding italic, and is also available as a variable font in both upright and italic variants. The character set covers Latin Extended and Latin Extended Additional, making it suitable for a broad range of Western European and Vietnamese languages. Its proportions and weight range suit it to editorial, signage and interface work where legibility across sizes is a priority.
foundry_blurb: Tokotype offer Nomina in two purchasing options: the Uprights and the Italics as separate families, alongside Variable and Variable Italic versions. Specimen settings showcase the typeface in architectural and technical contexts, demonstrating its performance across long-form text and short display settings alike.

name: Maleo
classification_notes: The letterforms shown across the specimen suggest a humanist sans-serif construction with optical warmth; the broad weight range and variable axis support point to a contemporary text and display family in that tradition.
summary: A nine-weight humanist sans-serif from Tokotype, available in static and variable formats with full italic coverage and broad Latin character support.
description: The family runs from Thin to Black across upright and italic styles, with variable font versions for both axes. Character coverage extends through Latin Extended-A, Latin Extended-B and Latin Extended Additional, making it well-suited to multilingual European typography. Each style is sold individually at a flat rate, giving buyers flexibility across the full weight range.
foundry_blurb: Tokotype presents Maleo as a versatile sans-serif family available in nine weights with matching italics, complemented by variable font versions covering both upright and italic axes. The typeface includes extensive glyph coverage across Latin and extended Latin unicode ranges.

name: Gramatika
classification_notes: The upright and italic families, variable axes, and glyph specimen suggest a clean geometric sans construction with consistent stroke widths and rational letterforms typical of the geometric sans genre.
summary: A geometric sans-serif from Tokotype offered in eight weights with matching italics, variable fonts and broad Latin and Cyrillic coverage.
description: Available in eight weights from ExtraLight to Black with corresponding italics, the family also ships as variable fonts covering both upright and italic axes. Glyph coverage extends across Latin Extended and Cyrillic, making it suited to multilingual branding and editorial work. Specimen usage skews towards packaging and product identity contexts.
foundry_blurb: Tokotype offer Gramatika in upright and italic families across eight static weights, complemented by variable font versions for both styles. The typeface supports an extensive character set including Latin Extended and Cyrillic scripts.

name: Fonetika
classification_notes: Clean, even stroke weight and geometric construction are evident across the specimen; the upright and italic families suggest a contemporary geometric sans-serif with variable font support.
summary: A geometric sans-serif from Tokotype with eight weights, matching italics and variable font versions, covering Latin Extended and Cyrillic.
description: The family spans ExtraLight to Black in both upright and italic cuts, with variable font counterparts for each. Specimen copy draws on Jakarta's public transit context, suggesting a design suited to signage, wayfinding and UI work. Language coverage extends across Latin Extended and Cyrillic, broadening its range for international use.
foundry_blurb: Fonetika is offered by Tokotype in individual weight purchases at $30 per style, covering uprights and italics from ExtraLight to Black, plus variable upright and variable italic versions. The specimen is set around Jakarta's MRT and KRL commuter rail system, framing the typeface within an urban transit context.

name: Aksen
classification_notes: Aksen displays clean, structured letterforms consistent with a geometric sans-serif approach, with no visible humanist or grotesque idiosyncrasies evident from the specimen and glyph set.
summary: A geometric sans-serif from Indonesian foundry Tokotype, available in seven weights with italics and a variable font axis spanning the full weight range.
description: Available in seven weights from Light to Black with matching italics, Aksen also ships as a variable font in both upright and italic variants, making it flexible for screen and print contexts alike. The glyph set covers Latin Extended-A and -B alongside Latin Extended Additional, giving it broad coverage for European and Vietnamese languages. The specimen positions it for aviation branding and long-form editorial settings, suggesting a typeface suited to neutral, functional environments.
foundry_blurb: Tokotype offer Aksen as a complete upright and italic family across seven weights, complemented by variable font versions for both styles. Individual styles are priced at $30 each, and the family includes extensive Latin Extended coverage.

name: Sinar Grotesk
classification_notes: A contemporary grotesque sans-serif with a broad weight range and variable font support, following conventions of neutral European grotesque tradition.
summary: A broad grotesque family from Tokotype spanning eight weights with italics and variable font support, covering an extensive Latin character set.
description: Eight upright weights from ExtraLight to Black are matched with a full set of italics, and the family is available as variable fonts for both upright and italic axes. Glyph coverage extends across Latin Extended-A, Latin Extended-B and Latin Extended Additional, accommodating a wide range of European and Vietnamese languages. Priced per weight, the family suits editorial, branding and broadcast contexts where versatility across weights matters.
foundry_blurb: Tokotype offer Sinar Grotesk in individual weight purchases across eight uprights and eight italics, alongside variable font versions for both upright and italic axes. The specimen copy presents a fictional radio station, Sinar Radio FM 98.2, to demonstrate the typeface across broadcast, editorial and digital contexts.

name: Leksikal Sans
classification_notes: Leksikal Sans presents upright and italic styles across seven weights with variable font support, consistent with a contemporary humanist sans-serif design intended for editorial and body text use.
summary: A seven-weight humanist sans with matching italics and variable font support, covering an extensive Latin character set.
description: Leksikal Sans spans seven weights from Light to Black, each with a corresponding italic, and is offered as a variable font for both upright and italic axes. The character set extends through Latin Extended-A, Extended-B and Latin Extended Additional, giving broad coverage for European and Vietnamese languages. Suited to editorial, branding and text work where a clean, adaptable sans is required.
foundry_blurb: Tokotype offer Leksikal Sans as a full upright and italic family across seven weights, alongside variable font versions for both axes, with specimen settings demonstrating the typeface across long-form editorial and packaging contexts.

name: Makro
classification_notes: A sans-serif with flared terminals and strong contrast between structured letterforms and organic details, designed primarily for display use across multiple widths and weights.
summary: A multi-width display sans with flared terminals and seven weights, available as a variable font with weight and width axes.
description: Makro spans Condensed, Normal and Extended widths across seven weights from Light to Black, with full italic coverage and variable font versions for both upright and italic styles. Its character comes from the contrast between geometric structure and organic, flared terminals, giving it a lively presence suited to headlines, titles and large-format graphics. Version 4.0 introduced variable weight and width axes, making the family adaptable across a wide range of display applications.
foundry_blurb: Tokotype describe Makro as a versatile sans-serif family reinterpreted with quirky traits, offered in three distinct widths and seven weights. Its defining characteristic is the bold contrast between structured letterforms and organic, flared terminals. Designed primarily for large display use such as out-of-home graphics, headlines and titles, the latest version adds variable weight and width axes for seamless adaptability across diverse design needs.

name: Frasa
classification_notes: The specimen imagery and editorial tone suggest a refined serif with classical proportions suited to long-form text and luxury branding, consistent with transitional serif classification.
summary: A six-weight serif family with variable font support, designed for editorial and luxury-adjacent contexts such as stationery, hospitality branding and long-form print.
description: Frasa offers six weights from Regular to Black, each with matching italics, alongside variable upright and italic versions that allow continuous weight interpolation. The specimen leans heavily into grand hotel aesthetics, positioning the typeface for stationery, hospitality design and editorial use. Latin Extended coverage is broad, supporting a wide range of European languages.
foundry_blurb: Tokotype present Frasa through evocative imagery of handwritten postcards, grand hotel lobbies and fountain-pen correspondence, framing it as a typeface suited to contexts where detail and a sense of unhurried elegance matter. The family includes upright and italic styles across six weights, plus variable font versions of both.
